---
title: "Draft Data Science Report"
author: "Your Name"
format: html
---

## Introduction

This report examines species count data. The purpose of the analysis is not fully developed yet.

## Data

The dataset comes from a simple CSV file included in the repository. It contains counts of different species.

## Analysis

```{r}
# Load data
data <- read.csv("../data/species_counts.csv")

# Calculate total count
total <- sum(data$count)

# Print result
total

```
Discussion

The results show differences among species, but the interpretation is unclear.
The data shows counts of four different tress. The total number of trees is 45, with pine being the most abundant type of tree, followed by oak, maple, and birch. These types of trees are found in Northern Hardwood and Transition Hardwood environments. A higher abundance of pine vs maple and birch suggest a warmer environment, likely Transition Hardwood.
